item.title: US-Iran Ceasefire to Fuel Asia Relief Rally, Strategists Say
item.description: Donald Trump’s announcement of a two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran brings welcome relief to Asian markets, according to strategists. Equities should see strong rallies across the region in the near term, they say.

item.title: North Korea Fires Projectile After South Korean Leader’s Apology
item.description: North Korea fired an unidentified projectile on Wednesday, South Korea’s military said, just days after South Korean President Lee Jae Myung expressed regret over an incident involving drones that crossed the border into the North’s airspace.

item.title: Treasuries Rise as Ceasefire Spurs Oil Drop, Fed Rate-Cut Hopes
item.description: Short-dated notes led a jump in Treasuries after the US-Iran ceasefire pummeled oil prices and fueled hopes for the Federal Reserve to resume interest rate cuts.

item.title: S. Korea Posts Record Current Account Surplus on Chip Exports
item.description: South Korea posted its biggest current account surplus on record in February as booming semiconductor exports lifted the goods balance before the Iran war began to ripple through energy markets and global shipping.

item.title: BofA Banker Bettamio to Be CEO Candidate for Brazil’s B3
item.description: Bank of America Corp.’s Alexandre Bettamio is among candidates being considered for chief executive officer of Brazilian stock and derivatives exchange B3 SA, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: SEC Fines Surge on Biden Enforcements Now Shifting Under Trump
item.description: US Securities and Exchange Commission fines and investor relief doubled to $17.9 billion in the 2025 fiscal year, a surge bolstered by enforcement actions at the end of the Biden administration that likely won’t continue as priorities shift under President Donald Trump.

item.title: Japan’s Real Wages Rise Most Since 2021 to Keep BOJ on Hike Path
item.description: Japanese workers’ wages adjusted for inflation rose at the fastest pace since 2021, backing the case for the Bank of Japan to consider a rate hike as soon as this month.

item.title: China Bank Stocks Emerge as Haven as Iran War Jolts Markets
item.description: Chinese bank shares have outpaced the broader market since the war in Iran broke out, with attractive dividend yields and improving earnings prospects likely to provide further tailwinds, according to analysts.

item.title: Pro-Iran Group Takes Credit for Cyberattacks on Chime, Pinterest
item.description: A pro-Iranian cybercrime group has claimed responsibility for cyberattacks on Chime Financial Inc. and Pinterest Inc. that knocked the websites of both companies offline.

item.title: Japanese Stocks Jump After Trump Agrees to Iran Ceasefire
item.description: Japanese equities jumped after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire that’s expected to halt the American-Israeli military campaign in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Centerbridge’s KIK Posts 50% Drop in Earnings Amid Plant Issues
item.description: Centerbridge Partners-backed KIK Custom Products reported a 50% drop in fourth quarter earnings compared to a year earlier, in part as production facility inefficiencies resulted in lower volumes and higher costs for the maker of household cleaning products.

item.title: Oil Slides, Stock Futures Rise on Trump Ceasefire: Markets Wrap
item.description: Crude oil slumped and US equity-index futures advanced after President Donald Trump agreed to suspend bombing of Iran for two weeks, offering markets a reprieve from volatility driven by the six-week Middle East conflict.

item.title: Gold Climbs After Trump Agrees to Two-Week Ceasefire in Iran
item.description: Gold rose after US President Donald Trump said he had agreed to a two-week extension to finalize talks on ending the war in Iran.

item.title: Oil Slumps Below $100 as Trump Delays Iran Attacks for Two Weeks
item.description: Oil plunged below $100 a barrel after US President Donald Trump postponed by two weeks his threat to attack civilian infrastructure across Iran, as negotiators inched closer to a ceasefire deal that will see Tehran reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Fed’s Jefferson Says Rates Well Positioned Amid Uncertainty
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson said the Iran war will stoke uncertainty and push US inflation higher in the near term, though the central bank’s policy setting remains appropriate.

item.title: First Brands Judge Delays Sale of 12 Brands to Allow Late Bid
item.description: Bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands Group must give a last-minute bidder a chance to buy some of its most-recognizable brands, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.

item.title: Musk Wants OpenAI Nonprofit to Get Any Trial Winnings From Suit
item.description: Elon Musk said any damages he may win during his upcoming trial against OpenAI should be awarded to the artificial intelligence startup’s nonprofit, rather than to himself.

item.title: Oncoclinicas Mulls Seeking Emergency Protection From Creditors in Brazil
item.description: Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA is considering seeking emergency protection from creditors as financial pressures mount, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Fertilizer Prices Are Still Soaring
item.description: Who makes money from rising prices?

item.title: Coinbase Plans Australia Expansion After Securing AFSL License
item.description: Coinbase Global Inc., the largest US cryptocurrency exchange, said it plans to expand into equity trading and payments in Australia after receiving a financial services license.

item.title: FTSE Confirms Vietnam’s Entry to Emerging Cohort in September
item.description: Vietnam’s stocks will be included in FTSE Russell’s secondary emerging markets grouping from September, boosting their visibility among global investors and paving the way for capital inflows.

item.title: Bond Investors Brace for Selloff as Trump’s Iran Deadline Nears
item.description: Bond investors who’ve seen losses on US Treasuries mount amid the unfolding war in the Middle East are positioning for even more declines as President Donald Trump’s deadline looms for Iran to agree a ceasefire deal.

item.title: Foreign Tanker Hauls Fuel From Washington State to California
item.description: A foreign-flagged tanker is sailing from Washington State to California, one of the first non-US vessels to bring fuel to the region from another US state after the Trump administration waived Jones Act maritime restrictions last month.

item.title: Super Micro Launches Internal Probe of Server Sales to China
item.description: Super Micro Computer Inc. said a committee of its independent directors has hired a law firm and is investigating the circumstances surrounding an indictment last month of two employees and a contractor over the sale of servers to China.

item.title: US Regulators Propose Overhaul for Anti-Money-Laundering Rules
item.description: US regulators unveiled a plan Tuesday to overhaul rules intended to prevent money laundering, a move likely to be cheered by Wall Street as banks continue to rack up wins in their push for policy changes under the Trump administration.

item.title: Goldman, JPMorgan Traders Map Stock Outcomes as War Risks Rise
item.description: Some of Wall Street’s largest trading desks are mapping an array of market outcomes tied to the Iran conflict, as investors grapple with uncertainty over the prospects for a ceasefire.

item.title: ‘Freak Out’ Indicator Soars to Record With War Sparking Trader Anxiety
item.description: Investors wary of being wrong-footed by twists and turns in the Iran war are trading stocks at a record pace, one market measure shows.

item.title: Macro Traders Slump Most in March as War Squeezes Hedge Funds
item.description: Macro hedge funds struggled in March as the war in the Middle East upended inflation expectations, leading to steep losses at many of the industry’s largest firms.

item.title: Vale Advances Oman Maintenance Shutdowns to Counter War Impacts
item.description: Vale SA, the world’s top iron ore producer, has brought forward a scheduled maintenance shutdown of its two pellet plants in Oman as a strategy to mitigate potential impacts from the Iran war, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chicago Atlantic Targets Emerging Markets in Private Credit Push
item.description: Chicago Atlantic is moving into private credit markets in the developing world, seeking to seize on increased demand as investors pull money out of similar funds in the US.
